stations.name: Lake James
stations.label: Percent of full pool (Duke Energy's own live figure)
stations.official_message: On May 1, 2026, the Catawba-Wateree River Basin entered Stage 2 of the Low Inflow Protocol (LIP) due to significantly below average rainfall in the area for the past several months and very low stream flows. As a result, lake levels are declining, and recreation flow schedules have been suspended as required under Stage 2 of the LIP. The Catawba-Wateree Drought Management Advisory Group (DMAG), which consists of representatives from Duke Energy, large water users, and federal and state resource agencies, is closely following conditions in the basin. As these dry weather conditions continue, the community is asked to limit water use and to conserve energy which also saves water. Residents who withdraw water from a Duke Energy lake for irrigation purposes are required to limit their water withdrawals to no more than two days a week (i.e., Tuesdays and Saturdays) and only use the minimum amount necessary. To ensure the safety of boaters and protect property, some boat ramps may close temporarily due to declining lake levels. Any closures will be listed on the Duke Energy Lakeview app and Duke-Energy.com/Lakes. Updated lake levels are available any time at www.duke-energy.com/lakes, by calling 800.829.5253, or on the Duke Energy Lake View mobile app.

stations.name: Nantahala Lake
stations.label: Reservoir surface elevation (Duke Energy)
stations.official_message: Duke Energy has scheduled a Nantahala River Bypass release below the Nantahala Dam for Friday, July 24, 2026. Flow on Friday will be 250 cfs and the spillway gate will be partially opened from about 12:15 PM to 5:00 PM or later to ensure the target flow is at the Whiteoak Creek confluence between 4:00 PM and 7:00 PM. WARNING: High flows, increased debris and the natural environment of the bypass can create dangerous conditions and is recommended for advanced paddlers only. Fishermen should be particularly aware of increases in flows and rapidly rising water. For information on current lake levels and conditions, please visit duke-energy.com/lakes, call 800.829.5253, or the Duke Energy Lake View mobile app.

stations.name: Tanasee Creek Lake
stations.label: Percent of full pool (Duke Energy's own live figure)
stations.official_message: On July 16, 2026, the Tuckasegee River Basin entered Stage 1 of the Low Inflow Protocol (LIP) due to significantly below average rainfall in the area for the past several months and very low stream flows. As a result, lake levels are declining, and recreation flow schedules have been reduced as required under Stage 1 of the LIP. As these dry weather conditions continue, the community is asked to limit water use and to conserve energy which also saves water. To ensure the safety of boaters and protect property, some boat ramps may close temporarily due to declining lake levels. Any closures will be listed on the Duke Energy Lakeview app and Duke-Energy.com/Lakes. Updated lake levels are available any time at www.duke-energy.com/lakes, by calling 800.829.5253, or on the Duke Energy Lake View mobile app.

stations.name: Cedar Cliff Lake
stations.label: Percent of full pool (Duke Energy's own live figure)
stations.official_message: The primary generating unit at Cedar Cliff hydro station on the East Fork of the Tuckasegee River is offline to complete necessary improvements to maintain reliable and efficient energy service for our customers. A spillway gate will be opened, as needed, to manage reservoir levels. Duke Energy will also be utilizing the spillway gate to provide the recreational releases for the 2026 season. Traditionally, releases have occurred every Wednesday, Thursday and Saturday. For 2026, releases will be on Tuesday, Thursday, and Saturday to avoid consecutive days of potentially warm water releases through the spillway gate. This ensures we protect the aquatic life in the river that so many enjoy. Dangerous high flow conditions can exist downstream of Cedar Cliff hydro station and will be significantly higher during rainfall events. Updated lake levels are available any time at duke-energy.com/lakes, by calling 800.829.5253, or on the Duke Energy Lake View mobile app.

stations.name: Lake Tillery
stations.label: Percent of full pool (Duke Energy's own live figure)
stations.official_message: Due to the lack of rainfall and very low stream flows in the Yadkin-Pee Dee River Basin, reservoir levels are lower than normal. Duke Energy is minimizing hydro station releases while staying in compliance with continuous minimum flow and water quality requirements. For information on current lake levels and conditions, please visit duke-energy.com/lakes, download the Duke Energy Lake View mobile app or call 800-829-5253.
